meanings.meaning: 登山运动员；阿尔卑斯登山家
meanings.usage: 特指擅长攀登阿尔卑斯山脉的专业登山者
用于描述某领域最早进行探索、开创先例的人或事物
可指山的最高点，也可引申为最高级别的官方会议
正式用语，指经过努力实现目标或达到某个水平
书面语，强调风险极高、可能造成严重伤害的
通常因恐惧、寒冷或厌恶而不自觉地颤抖，常与at搭配
此处指主动招致不好的事，也可表示追求异性、博取关注
强调单个的、没有其他伴随的
正式用语，形容地区或人极度贫穷的状态
复合形容词，由名词+过去分词构成，表充满某个不好的事物的
可形容物品质量差，也可形容人的言行粗鲁

meanings.meaning: 有（值得骄傲的东西）；吹嘘
meanings.usage: 中性义指某地/某人拥有某个好的事物，贬义指夸耀、说大话
指归某个教堂管辖的当地居民
强调情况没有例外、总是如此
固定搭配为be accustomed to (doing) sth，表习惯于某事

phrases.usage: 用于表示两个事物按比例同步变化，意为“越……就越……”

examples.en: The more difficult it is, the more highly it is regarded.
examples.zh: 路线越难，就越被人们看重。

examples.en: The more you practice, the more fluent your English will be.
examples.zh: 你练习得越多，英语就会越流利。

phrases.usage: 指某个领域的初创、探索阶段，意为“在开创初期”

examples.en: In the pioneering days however, this was not the case at all.
examples.zh: 然而在登山运动的初创时期，情况完全不是这样。

examples.en: In the pioneering days of the Internet, few people could imagine its popularity today.
examples.zh: 在互联网发展初期，很少有人能想象到它如今的普及程度。

phrases.phrase: go out of one's way to do sth
phrases.usage: 意为“特地、格外努力做某事；刻意做某事”

examples.en: They did not go out of their way to court such excitement.
examples.zh: 他们并不是刻意追求这种刺激。

examples.en: The host went out of his way to make every guest feel at home.
examples.zh: 主人费尽心思让每位客人都感觉自在。

phrases.usage: 意为“切断、隔绝与……的联系”，常用被动形式

examples.en: Alpine villages tended to be impoverished settlements cut off from civilization by the high mountains.
examples.zh: 阿尔卑斯山区的村庄大多是贫穷的村落，被高山隔绝了与文明世界的联系。

examples.en: The small town was cut off from the outside world due to the heavy snow.
examples.zh: 由于大雪，这个小镇和外界失去了联系。

phrases.usage: 意为“（就着饮料）把食物送下；冲洗”

examples.en: All washed down with coarse wine.
examples.zh: 所有食物都得就着粗劣的葡萄酒咽下。

examples.en: You need some water to wash down the medicine.
examples.zh: 你需要点水来把药送服下去。

phrases.usage: 意为“习惯于……”，to是介词，后接名词或动名词

examples.en: For men accustomed to eating seven-course dinners and sleeping between fine linen sheets at home.
examples.zh: 对于那些习惯了在家吃七道菜的正餐、睡细亚麻床单的人来说。

examples.en: She is accustomed to jogging for half an hour every morning.
examples.zh: 她习惯了每天早上慢跑半小时。

grammar.title: the + 比较级，the + 比较级结构
grammar.definition: 表示两个过程按比例同时增减，即前者变化的同时后者也随之发生相应变化
grammar.structure: the + 形容词/副词比较级 + 句子，the + 形容词/副词比较级 + 句子
grammar.usage: 用于说明两个变量的正相关或负相关关系，前半句相当于条件状语，后半句是结果

examples.en: The more difficult it is, the more highly it is regarded.
examples.zh: 路线越难，就越被人们看重。

examples.en: The earlier you start, the sooner you will finish the task.
examples.zh: 你开始得越早，完成任务就越快。

grammar.title: 过去分词作后置定语
grammar.definition: 过去分词放在被修饰的名词之后，相当于一个被动语态的定语从句，表被动或完成的含义
grammar.structure: 名词 + 过去分词短语
grammar.usage: 当定语内容较长时，常把过去分词短语后置，避免句子头重脚轻

examples.en: Alpine villages tended to be impoverished settlements cut off from civilization by the high mountains.
examples.zh: 阿尔卑斯山区的村庄大多是贫穷的村落，被高山隔绝了与文明世界的联系。

examples.en: The book written by my professor has been sold out nationwide.
examples.zh: 我教授写的那本书已经在全国售罄了。

original.en: It is true that during their explorations they often faced difficulties and dangers of the most perilous nature, equipped in a manner which would make a modern climber shudder at the thought, but they did not go out of their way to court such excitement.
original.zh: 诚然，在探险过程中他们经常面临极度的困难和危险，他们的装备简陋到现代登山者一想就会不寒而栗，但他们并不是刻意追求这种刺激。

imitations.en: It is true that learning a new language takes a lot of time and effort, but the benefits it brings are priceless.
imitations.zh: 诚然，学习一门新语言需要投入大量时间和精力，但它带来的好处是无价的。

imitations.en: It is true that the team has made great progress this season, but they still have a long way to go to win the championship.
imitations.zh: 诚然，这支队伍本赛季已经取得了很大进步，但要赢得冠军他们还有很长的路要走。

sentencePatterns.pattern: It is hard for sb to do sth nowadays

original.en: It is hard for us to realize nowadays how difficult it was for the pioneers.
original.zh: 如今我们很难想象当初的先驱者们面临的是怎样的困难。

imitations.en: It is hard for young people nowadays to imagine how life was like without smartphones.
imitations.zh: 现在的年轻人很难想象没有智能手机的生活是什么样的。

imitations.en: It is hard for city dwellers nowadays to experience the quiet life in the countryside.
imitations.zh: 如今的城市居民很难体验到乡村的宁静生活。

sentencePatterns.pattern: For sb accustomed to..., sth must have been very hard indeed

original.en: For men accustomed to eating seven-course dinners and sleeping between fine linen sheets at home, the change to the Alps must have been very hard indeed.
original.zh: 对于那些习惯了在家吃七道菜的正餐、睡细亚麻床单的人来说，到阿尔卑斯山的这种生活转变肯定非常艰难。

imitations.en: For people accustomed to working in air-conditioned offices, doing physical work outdoors in summer must have been very hard indeed.
imitations.zh: 对于习惯了在空调办公室工作的人来说，夏天在户外做体力活肯定非常难熬。

imitations.en: For children accustomed to being taken care of by their parents, living alone in a new city must have been very hard indeed.
imitations.zh: 对于习惯了被父母照顾的孩子来说，独自在陌生城市生活肯定非常不容易。
